blocks:
Sūrat Waḍ-Ḍuḥā

Meccan, its number of verses is eleven verses, without dispute بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالضُّحى ** (1) By the morning brightness He swore by the time of the sun's elevation. (٢) وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا سَجى ** (2) and the night when it is still And by the night when its people are at rest and its darkness settles. (٣) ما وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ ** (3) Your Lord has neither forsaken you He did not cut you off with the cutting off of one who bids farewell. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: ما وَدَعك (with the lightening of the د) meaning He did not leave you, and nor hated [you] (وَما قَلى) meaning He did not hate you (وما أبغضك). Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: That was because Gabriel delayed coming to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the first Surah revealed was اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ ** (Recite in the Name of your Lord who created). Then he delayed coming to him, so Khadijah said: "Perhaps your Lord has abandoned you, so He does not send [messages] to you." So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed: ما وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَما قَلى (Your Lord has neither forsaken you, nor hated [you]). And in al-Jawami': It is narrated that the revelation was withheld from him for some days, so the polytheists said: "Indeed, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has been forsaken by his Lord and He has hated him." So [the Surah] was revealed.

(٤) وَلَلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَكَ مِنَ الْأُولى (1) (4) And the Hereafter will surely be better for you than the former [life]. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "It means the Return (الكرّة) is the Hereafter for the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace)."

(٥)** وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضى ** (5) Soon your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied. He said: "He will give you from Paradise until you are satisfied."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), He said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) entered upon Fatimah, and she was wearing a cloak made of camel hair (ثُلَّة الإبل). She was grinding grain with her hand and nursing her child. The eyes of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) teared up when He saw her. He said: "O my daughter, hasten the bitterness of this world for the sweetness of the Hereafter, for Allah has revealed to me: وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضى. (5) Soon your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied."

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from him (peace be upon him), is a similar narration, and in it, after His saying "for the sweetness of the Hereafter," it states: So she said: "O Messenger of Allah, all praise belongs to Allah for His blessings (نعمائه) and thanks for His favors (آلائه)." Then Allah revealed: وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضى. (5) Soon your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied.

And in al-Majma', Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "My grandfather was satisfied that no monotheist (موحّد) remains in the Fire." And it is narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Hanafiyyah that he said: "O people of Iraq, you claim that the most hopeful verse (أرجى آية) in the Book of Allah, the Exalted, is يا عِبادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا (O My servants who have transgressed against their souls...) [the rest of] the verse. But we, the People of the House (أهل البيت), say that the most hopeful verse in the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضى (5) Soon your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied. By Allah, it is the intercession (الشفاعة), so that He may give us [the right to intercede] concerning the people of Lā Ilāha Illā Allāh (There is no god but Allah), until my Lord says: 'I am satisfied.'"

footnote (1) Meaning that the reward of the Hereafter and the everlasting bliss therein is better for you than the transient world.

(٦) أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيماً فَآوى. (6) Did He not find you an orphan, and shelter you? (٧) وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا فَهَدى. (7) And find you astray, and guide you? (٨)** وَوَجَدَكَ عائِلاً فَأَغْنى ** (8) And find you needy, and enrich you? This is an enumeration of the blessings He bestowed upon him, alerting him that just as He showed kindness to him in the past, He will show kindness in the future. Its apparent meaning is clear.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): يَتِيماً ** ("an orphan") means unique (فرداً), having no equal (لا مثل لك) among the creatures (في المخلوقين). فَآوى ** ("and shelter you") means He sheltered the people (النّاس) toward you. And ضَالًّا ** ("astray") means among a people (في قوم) who did not know your merit (فضلك), so He guided them to you. And عائِلاً ** ("needy") means you sustain (تعول) nations (اقواماً) with knowledge (بالعلم), so Allah enriched them through you.

And al-Qummi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): [He said] what is similar in meaning. And al-Qummi said: The orphan (اليتيم) is he who has no equal (لا مثل له). For this reason, the unique pearl (الدّرة اليتيمة) is named so because it has no equal (لا مثل لها). ** وَوَجَدَكَ عائِلاً فَأَغْنى ("And find you needy, and enrich you"). He said: So He enriched you with the Revelation (بالوحي), so you do not ask anyone (أحداً) about anything (عن شيء). وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا فَهَدى ** ("And find you astray, and guide you"). He said: He found you astray (ضَالًّا) among a people (في قوم) who did not know the merit of your prophethood (فضل نبوّتك), so Allah guided them through you.

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a tradition concerning the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets (peace be upon them): أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيماً فَآوى ("Did He not find you an orphan, and shelter you?"). He says: Did He not find you solitary (وحيداً), so the people (الناس) sheltered toward you. وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا ("And find you astray"). Meaning: among your people (عند قومك). فَهَدى ** ("And guide you"). Meaning: Allah guided them to the knowledge (معرفتك) of you. وَوَجَدَكَ عائِلاً فَأَغْنى ** ("And find you needy, and enrich you"). He says: By making your supplication (دعاءك) answered (مستجاباً).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): My Lord bestowed favor (منّ) upon me, and He is worthy of the favor (وهو أهل المنّ). And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked why the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) was orphaned (اوتم) from his parents (عن أبويه). He replied: So that no creature (لمخلوق) would have a claim (حقّ) upon him.

(٩) فَأَمَّا الْيَتِيمَ فَلا تَقْهَرْ ** (9) Then as for the orphan, do not oppress him. Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, do not treat unjustly (لا تظلم). And the address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for the people. (١٠) وَأَمَّا السَّائِلَ فَلا تَنْهَرْ ** (10) And as for the beggar, do not chide him. Meaning, do not drive away (لا تطرد) (1). (١١) وَأَمَّا بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّثْ ** (11) And as for the blessing of your Lord, relate it. He said: Relate what Allah has sent down upon you and commanded you with, such as the prayer (الصلاة), the poor-rate (الزكاة), the fast (الصوم), the pilgrimage (الحجّ), and the guardianship (الولاية); and relate what Allah has favored you with.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Its meaning is: relate what Allah has given you, favored you with, provided you with, shown kindness to you, and guided you.

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Husayn ibn 'Ali عليهما السلام, he said: He commanded him to relate what Allah bestowed upon him concerning His religion (دينه).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: فَحَدِّثْ ** (relate it) concerning His religion (بدينه), and what Allah gave him, and what He bestowed upon him.

And narrated from him عليه السلام, he said: When Allah bestows a blessing upon His servant and it becomes apparent upon him, he is named the beloved of Allah (حبيب الله), relating the blessing of Allah (محدّثاً بنعمة الله). And when Allah bestows a blessing upon His servant and it does not become apparent upon him, he is named the hated of Allah (بغيض الله), denying the blessing of Allah (مكذّباً بنعمة الله). The reward for reciting it preceded in Surat ash-Shams.

(1) And do not turn him away if he comes to you asking, for you were poor.
